description_long: This rice cooker features a rounded, pot-like body with a matching domed lid, both rendered in a uniform, matte brown color reminiscent of traditional clay cookware. The lid is topped with a small, circular knob for easy removal, and the pot itself is equipped with two broad, horizontally aligned handles on opposite sides for secure gripping. The base of the cooker is slightly flared and cylindrical, with a prominent opening at the front, likely for ventilation or access to a heating element. The overall design is simple and functional, with no visible electronic controls or decorative elements. The surface appears slightly textured, suggesting a handcrafted or earthenware finish, and the object is compact enough to be easily portable.
description: A brown, rounded rice cooker with a domed lid, side handles, and a cylindrical base featuring a front opening. The surface is matte and slightly textured, evoking traditional clay cookware.
description_view_0: Front view showing the domed lid, two side handles, and a prominent front opening at the base.
description_view_1: Rear view with the lid and handles visible, but the base opening is not present; the back is smooth and uninterrupted.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the symmetry of the handles and the continuous curve of the pot and base.
description_view_3: Opposite rear view, similar to view 1, with the back of the lid and base visible and no opening.
attribute_lid_design: The lid is domed, made of the same brown clay material as the body, fully removable, and features a small, round knob at the center. No vents, windows, or locks are visible.
attribute_handle_type: Two fixed, horizontally aligned handles are integrated into the upper sides of the pot, made of the same brown clay material.
